sentences.text: Hey, Jody.
Can you hear me?
All right.
Hey, I can hear you.
How are you doing today?
What's going on?
I'm doing well.
How are you doing?
Not too bad, busy week.
Uh But uh thanks so much for taking the time to connect with
me.
No, I appreciate you taking the time to connect with me.
Unfortunately, we have almost reached the end of the week.
So, um hopefully this will be a quick and easy call.
Um So as you know, I had reached out to try to get those contracts canceled because both of those projects have painfully, painfully stalled.
And so um I apologize.
I was taken totally by surprise.

I don't, I don't remember if you were involved in our contracting process, but um the way that it typically works is I serve the contracts up for our CEO to sign and it is incredibly unusual that we will sign anything that has an automatic full term renewal.
Usually if we'll sign that it's like a month to month.
So when you said that I was like, oh my gosh, that is, I am blown away.
I can't believe that we did that.
So of course, I went back and took a look and I went, oh, yep.
No, there it is.

Um So I just had questions for you because I mean on, on the one property in particular the Bell, that one we are not even going to be managing after the end of April, that one is exiting our portfolio.
Um We divested that will be one of the last properties that we are completely divesting out of our Ohio portfolio.
So I just needed to try to figure out like, what, what are our options there?
What does that look like?
Yeah, great question and totally understand.
I think um it happens a lot more often than, than you probably imagine.
But yeah, the auto renew clause is in just about all our contracts.
So it does tend to take people by surprise.
Um Sorry to have surprised you there.
But uh what I wanted to do today is definitely talk to you about the options if the property is no longer going to be in your portfolio.
Um I was just checking out how the contract was written and I believe we have the contract such that it specifies the specific property.

Um And so since it specifies the bell, we should be able to just as soon as you have the official date for which it will no longer be in the Allied Management uh property portfolio, we should be able to terminate that contract uh because we're not gonna, you know, we're not looking to hold anybody over the coals because a property left the portfolio.
Right.
That wouldn't make any sense.
Yeah.
And that's what I expected when I talk to you because like I said, that would be so outside of the norm in this industry, I was like, there's got to be an exit for, for termination of, of management.
But yeah, I did want to talk to you about that.
Now, our date that we have on it right now just to, again, to total and utter transparency.
Both of these projects are owned by the same client.
And part of the challenge is is that they've never received funding um either for marketing.
So we've been continuing to cover these costs.
So we're trying to help them through while they try to determine if they're going to pull this thing in house until they can get it back on track or what they're going to do.
We've got a date that is set with them of the end of um April.
So April 30th is right now the exit date.
Um
I can go ahead and give that to you and I, I feel confident that that's gonna hold.
But if there's anything that shifts on that, I can definitely reach back out to you and let you
know.
Yeah, definitely, please do.
I think that would make this the easiest way to, to handle that as long as we can go in, you know, and confirm that the property is no longer under your management.
It's an easy out.
Absolutely
awesome.
Ok, so yeah, just uh know that that is our date if you, you need me to send that to you like in an email, let me know I can do that, but definitely
please do.
Yeah, actually my billing team just needs that in writing.
Ok, you got it.
I will send that in an email.
Um And then like I said, if anything shifts on that, I will definitely let you know, we do not expect it to.

And so then the other question that I had for you and I don't even know that we wanna open up this particular can of worms, but I had my backup plan ready in case you were like Jody, we cannot like terminate that for management.
I was gonna ask you, um, if we could identify another property that we needed to transfer that to, is that a possibility?
So the reason that I ask you that is because, um right now I'm not gonna do anything with four seaters, we're gonna leave that we're still, that was still under management.
We still hope that one's gonna move forward.
Um, but that one could exit as well.
I guess it doesn't matter because if that one access, it's also gonna access for management.
So that's
ok.
And if we do want to swap to swap something in that is also an option as well.
Um I guess I was, I was, you know, one of the things I wanted to understand is, is kind of where you were with either of these properties.
Um, you know, are we leasing them?
What are we doing to manage?
Comp apartment IQ, you know, does get the plug pulled.
What's the process right now?
Totally.
So for both of these properties, they're both in construction.
Um So there's no leasing.
There is right now.
The expected completion date of the bell has pushed to 2025.
Um Yeah, no, that one is, is painful.
Um And the expected completion date uh for cedars was supposed to be October of last year.
It is now looking like it will deliver sometime in the summer.
But every time we get closer, that thing pushes out again.
Yep.
And the problem on that one is that there was upheaval in the city of Gunter right in the middle of our build.
So like their whole, their whole governing body has kind of changed out up there and we've been caught in the middle of that.
So that's been surprising.
Um So if anything changes on that one, if we end up giving back management of that one, I will let you know, but I will send you an email on the bell.
The reason that we had went with apartment IQ for these particular properties is because we have Axio Metris, but we don't have the markets um in Axio that these particular properties sat in.
And so for horse cedars, it was really weird because we have Dallas.
But the way that Axio is set up, Gunter is in a different city or is in a different um
sub market and we did not have a license for that one.
And so it made sense for us to get something in there that would help give us some better visibility that we couldn't get out of the co and then the same thing was true of Ohio.
We didn't have OX O for Ohio while we were there.
So with this lease up, it made sense.
But with, you know, the portfolio and like I said, we have divested out of Ohio completely.
What that will leave for us is coverage by OXO of every property except for four seaters at this point.
I see.
So that's kind of where we've been at and we had actually hoped to get out of Axio and shift business more over to apartment IQ.
And I'll tell you it's just because it's easier to use, right?
It's more intuitive for our on site team members than Axio, which is um you know, that that is a much more difficult from a user interface perspective.
I feel like a lot of those legacy tools kind of are they're lumbering right to get to what you need.
Yes.
And you feel like you have to have like an engineering degree just to be able to like figure out like how to interface with the thing.
And so it's been really prohibitive versus where apartment IQ is very simplified for our teams to go in and be able to see things quickly for themselves.
So we had actually explored getting out of our A I UM contract and it is pretty ironclad right now.

And so, yeah, so um there may be an opportunity um later this year for us to take a look again in advance of our contract where we can go ahead and, and give them the appropriate notification and then make a switch.
So just know that that's, it's not entirely off the table.
It's just unfortunately, on pause while we um make our way through our Axio commitment.
No.
Sure.
Um When does that, you know, when would that be approximately Q three or Q four towards Q
four?
I believe that it's actually up in like February 2025 but that means that we need to get to planning for whatever that change is gonna be typically by October.
So that if there's budget, you know, implications, which there always are, we know what they are and we can be prepared for that as we go into the New Year.

So by October, I would say we should be in a position to really um start to talk about that again and, and get um get something moving potentially because uh like I said really, we, we have seen that benefit for our on site team members.
This is a much more user friendly tool.
Um And even our owners, they've really um enjoyed it.
In fact, while I'm thinking of it, I have an owner that has asked for access uh to both of these properties while we still have them, you know, we still have to build up.
So um I can send that to you in the email as well.
What his, his email address is and what he needs to get.
Um access,
please do.
Yeah, let's get everybody in here as much as we can.
Uh I think if people like the tool, it, it, you know, we should give them access to it.
Uh Something else that I had up my sleeve to talk to you today if we were in a situation where we weren't changing, manage management, we kind of had to run out the contract.
Um I wanted to highlight some changes that we've made to IQ since you signed your last contract.
Um My understanding was that was almost a year ago.
Um And we've made a lot of changes to apartment IQ and just how people use it IQ the way, you know, and use it lives in this data tab.
Now, this is the usual stuff, right?
You've got your data tab and all these graphs here.
We added this market survey report which is supposed to speak more towards folks on the on site side, just because it's so much more familiar to them.
Uh In, in this particular orientation, we've also added additional metrics that you don't get in IQ today as it's contracted for you right now.
For instance, exposure metrics, leasing velocity on the flip side, availability, velocity, uh concessions, details and then collection dates.
And then down here, we have of course your pricing metrics based on either a minimum or average across these metrics.
Uh And then by bed type, if that's a more preferred way to ingest this information,
pause there.
Yeah, just to kind of get your thoughts here.
I'd be happy, you know, something that we have up our sleeves here when people tell us, hey, Phil apartment IQ isn't working for me.
I need to get out of this contract and we kind of have to tell them, look, I can't let you out of this contract.

It's just, you know, it's ironclad I will turn these features on so that folks can kind of see what the difference is and see if this drives a little bit more value in the context of uh not the bell, the one that we're keeping.
Um I just had ears.
Yeah.
Is it, is it worth it maybe to have this kind of functionality enabled or are we really looking to just kind of move away from IQ?
Um And, and I it's, it's a two fold question.
No, I think it's a great question.
And here's what I would say, Phil is that, you know, these owners that we are working with, they will contract with another company, right.
They're, they're gonna have management of their properties and they have a fairly good sized portfolio.
Um, and they've got other investors that they work with and I think that um we've started to get them
very interested.
They like the data.
That's an apartment IQ I think it's going to be great to give Curtis access.
He's a high decision maker in that organization.
He's one of the chief decision makers.
And I think that there's a huge possibility that even after management transitions, um we'll be happy to provide the management information for the new company.

If there's an opportunity for you to be able to, you know, re engage on that side of the house for that relationship because they do seem to really value the tool and I could see them on the other side with whoever they go with, um potentially wanting to retain it.
So, um if you know, if you want to go ahead and turn it on, I guess, said Curtis will be in there.
Um And I know that um again, he's a decision maker for it.
So I will leave that one to you.
Um I did, you know, on four cedars, that one is also stalled.
So, I mean, for us right now, there's, there's a low benefit on these, I think for the owners where the benefits coming in for them.
If I had to suspect why Curtis wants in given where we're at on these projects.
It's because they're trying to use this insight as they have to go back potentially for financing purposes.
Right.
Exactly.
So I have a feeling that's really where he's hoping that this data can help him.
So I'll leave that to you on if you think that that can help him in that process or not.

Um But um it is good to know so that for the future when we circle back on the conversation, I know that this would help our on site teams, we ask them to do, you know, obviously a similar exercise um every single month, but this is a really, really nice snapshot.
Yeah.
No, glad you think.
So.
That's super helpful context.

One more quick question, I'll have Jody just because we have in the past, given the, the right portfolio size, talked about buying folks out of the end of their contracts, you know, with competitors in the space.
Um What would be the size of the portfolio that's currently on axios?
Um
And, and that would probably be eligible for IQ potentially.
Yeah.
So the size of the portfolio that is, that's a great question because I don't know that I've looked at the numbers since we have invested.
Uh we just did it within the last two weeks, we um gave back nine properties in Ohio.
So that was a pretty big, pretty big impact to our overall count.
Um I wanna say right now where we're at is probably around the 4000 door, mark
4000 doors.
Ok.
Yeah.
All right.
That's, that's helpful to know.
Let me talk to you.
So I'll turn those features on, we'll get Curtis in.

Would you be comfortable with me reaching out to Curtis or would you mind email uh potentially introducing me to Curtis just so I can kind of put him in front of this and in front of our product team, uh as we're pulling out new things, we have a proforma tool that's coming to market.
And I think in the context of acquisitions on the context of underwriting uh and even preparing to enter new markets, we're seeing that it's going to be a great tool for folks.
So that's his realm, we feed back.
Yeah.
So what I will um ask on that is if you would wait until we are done with these to go ahead and do that, that would be fantastic if we're, once we've exited, feel free.
OK, fair enough.
That's totally fine with me.
Um And then one other thing I'm highlighting here is just the PDF S that updated as well with trend information concessions, information about when there are new concessions or ending concessions.
So just wanted to highlight we're driving forward pretty fast these days and, and adding a lot of things.
So hopefully uh this tool become something that is really helpful for your team down the road.
Uh But thank you so much Jody.
I think I really appreciate the context you gave me a lot of good information there and I think we have a plan going forward.
Perfect.
I will get you that email um both with confirmation of that day and then his email to go ahead and get him access.
Um And then like I said, I will keep you updated as we get closer to that date just to confirm that that's what's in place and what's happening.
And then um like I said, we'll keep everything as is with four seaters.
But if there's a change on that, I will reach back out on that one.
Terrific.
I'll turn on those features for four seaters and uh we'll we'll stay in touch.
All right.
Thank you.
I
appreciate it.
Thanks.
Take care.
Bye bye.
